(No.00223446, Development of object-oriented synthetic data generation and evaluation methods)

hero.title: Synthetic Spatio-Temporal Data Vault
hero.subtitle: SSTDV is a research project for generating and evaluation synthetic training data from the real-world spatiotemporal data.

features.heroSubtitle: Generate high-quality synthetic data from real-world spatio-temporal data, and verify quality through statistical and objective metrics.
features.generationSubtitle: Develops a principled framework for generating high-quality synthetic spatio-temporal data by explicitly integrating domain knowledge at every stage of the synthesis pipeline. Rather than relying solely on data-driven learning, the framework encodes physical constraints, inter-variable relationships, and task-specific objectives to produce synthetic data that is statistically faithful, structurally coherent, and purpose-aligned — across domains ranging from ocean dynamics to medical imaging.
features.knowledgeEncodingDesc:
• Knowledge encoding implements computable data structures to represent domain prior knowledge, including structure, dynamics, and physical constraints, of entities in spatio-temporal data.
  • Generative models are built to explicitly leverage this encoded knowledge, enabling principled and controllable synthetic data generation.

SoftwareBrainShapeToolKit — Brain MRI-based shape extraction
DiffAM — Spine CT-based anatomical modeling
VCM — Conditional brain MRI image generation
HF-GAN — High-fidelity unified brain MRI synthesis
Open DataNote: To request the full synthetic dataset, please contact eunhye0323@kaist.ac.kr.
features.relationModelingDesc:
• Relation modeling aims to identify how ocean variables are connected across time, space, and resolution. In SST analysis, this helps explain how past temperature changes, extreme high- and low-temperature events, and coarse-resolution ocean fields relate to future or high-resolution SST patterns.
  • To model these relationships, we use EVL-based SST forecasting to capture temporal extreme events and GAN-based SST downscaling to learn spatial relationships between coarse- and high-resolution ocean fields.

SoftwareKIOST-SST-Downscaling — GAN-based SST downscaling for spatial relation modeling
KIOST-SST-EVL — EVL-based SST forecasting for temporal extreme event modeling
Open DataKIOST-SST-EVL/data · KIOST-SST-Downscaling/data/OSTIA/peninsula
Note: Only a partial dataset is available on GitHub due to file size limitations. To request the full synthetic dataset, please contact tkkim@kiost.ac.kr.
features.dataAssimilationDesc:
• Data assimilation combines numerical model outputs and real observations to estimate a more reliable ocean state. It is especially useful when observations are sparse, noisy, or incomplete, because physical constraints can guide the reconstruction of missing or uncertain variables.
  • To address this, we combine 3D-Var data assimilation with deep learning to generate spatiotemporal ocean data that reflects both observational evidence and physically informed constraints.

SoftwareKIOST-3D-VAR-DA — 3D-Var data assimilation combined with deep learning for spatiotemporal ocean data generation
Open DataKIOST-3D-VAR-DA/data
Note: Only a partial dataset is available on GitHub due to file size limitations. To request the full synthetic dataset, please contact tkkim@kiost.ac.kr.
features.objectiveReinforcementDesc:
• Objective Synthesis focuses on goal-oriented generative modeling, where the synthesis process is explicitly conditioned on clinical objectives rather than relying purely on statistical learning. In the context of breast cancer surgical simulation, this means generating anatomically plausible post-operative MRIs that reflect specific surgical plans and patient-specific anatomy.
  • The model predicts the post-operative breast from the patient's pre-operative MRI and planned surgery type — conditioned on resection regions, implant location, and implant size. Trained on 1,483 cases of real paired pre- and post-operative studies, it captures complex morphological transformations induced by breast-conserving surgery and reconstruction, enabling physicians to visualize expected outcomes before surgery and empowering patients to participate in treatment decisions.

SoftwareDiffuAug — Breast MRI generation using Classifier-Free Diffusion Guidance with DDPM and DDIM sampling
features.evaluationSubtitle: Establishes a rigorous evaluation framework to validate synthetic 3D T1-weighted (T1-w) brain MRIs. Because generated medical images demand strict mathematical, biological, and clinical verification to ensure their reliability, we developed a comprehensive pipeline to systematically assess the data. This framework integrates a three-tier statistical analysis—comprising 1) Univariate Marginal Distributions, 2) Multivariate Correlations, and 3) Longitudinal Time-Series Trajectories—with a complementary 4) Expert Evaluation via a Visual Turing Test (Qualitative Assessment).
features.statisticalSimilarityDesc:
• Evaluates morphological features (e.g., volume, cortical thickness, surface area) extracted from distinct anatomical regions — measuring statistical distance between real and synthetic probability distributions across three dimensions.
  • Univariate Statistical Similarity: Assesses individual feature distributions using three core metrics
    • KS Test: Measures maximum CDF distance, confirming features follow the same probability distribution.
    • KL Divergence: Quantifies information loss when approximating real distributions; sensitive to tail mismatches for rare anatomical variations.
    • JS Divergence: Symmetric, bounded (0–1) metric providing a normalized score of overall structural similarity.
  • Multivariate Correlation Analysis: Evaluates preservation of multidimensional relationships inherent in brain anatomy
    • Feature Interdependencies: Computes Pearson/Spearman correlation matrices across variables in real and synthetic datasets.
    • Structural Covariance: Compares covariance matrices to confirm the model learned global anatomical structure, not just independent pixel intensities.
  • Longitudinal Evaluation via LMM: Verifies dynamic biological changes over time using a Linear Mixed-Effects Model
    • Longitudinal Structure: Disentangles subject-specific random effects from group-level fixed effects (e.g., time, age).
    • Trajectory Consistency: Compares LMM slopes and intercepts to confirm synthetic data reproduces chronological structural changes such as volume reduction due to aging or disease progression.

SoftwareEvalDistSim — 1D distribution similarity via KS test, KL divergence, and Jensen-Shannon divergence
EvalCorrSyn — Evaluation of multivariate distribution similarity in synthetic data
EvalSynLongD — Statistical similarity of real and synthetic longitudinal data (LMM, Cohen's d)
features.objectiveRelevanceDesc:
• CNN-based evaluation assesses whether synthetic brain imaging data possesses sufficient biological, anatomical, and clinical validity for use in AI model training and real research settings.
  • Visual & Structural Similarity: Evaluates whether synthetic images are visually and structurally similar to real brain images and whether CNN-extracted features are comparable between real and synthetic datasets.
  • Disease-Related Preservation: Assesses whether synthetic data preserves disease-related differences between patient and control groups across stages of disease progression.
features.turingTestTitle: Expert Evaluation via Visual Turing Test
features.turingTestDesc:
• Beyond mathematical and statistical validations, assessing the clinical and anatomical realism of synthetic medical images requires human expert judgment, implemented via a Visual Turing Test.
  • Clinical Realism Assessment: Domain experts (radiologists or neurologists) were presented with a blinded, randomized mix of real and synthetic 3D T1-w MRIs and tasked with classifying each scan as "real" or "synthetic."
  • Perceptual Fidelity Validation: If experts' classification accuracy approaches random chance (~50%), the synthetic MRIs are visually indistinguishable from authentic scans. Passing this test confirms a high degree of morphological accuracy and clinical viability without obvious artifacts.

resources.subtitle: Access project research, use cases, publications & patents, and docs.
resources.researchDesc: SSTDV's synthetic data generation and evaluation methodology.
resources.useCasesDesc: Validated use cases in brain imaging and breast cancer surgical simulation.
resources.publicationsDesc: Research outcomes from the SSTDV project.
resources.docsDesc: Open-source repositories from the SSTDV project.

useCases.heroSubtitle: Validated in two medical imaging domains, demonstrating the effectiveness of objective-reinforced synthetic data.
useCases.brainOverview: Virtual Brain is a framework that simulates longitudinal and chronic brain imaging changes associated with neurodegenerative diseases and generates synthetic brain imaging data that can be used for disease classification and prediction model development.
useCases.brainDataDesc: Brain imaging data from Dongguk University and the ADNI open dataset.
useCases.brainScenario1Desc:
Input: Current age, sex, diagnosis (Normal / MCI), MMSE score

Output:
  • Case 1 - Brain health maintained: Predicted MRIs at 3-year intervals (axial, coronal, sagittal views) generated from subjects maintaining Normal or MCI status, predicted MMSE score
  • Case 2 - Brain health not managed:
    • Predicted MRIs at 3-year intervals from subjects transitioning Normal → MCI, predicted MMSE score
    • Predicted MRIs at 3-year intervals from subjects transitioning MCI → AD, predicted MMSE score
useCases.brainScenario2Desc:
Input: Patient MRI, current age, sex, diagnosis (Normal / MCI), MMSE score

Output:
  • Case 1 - Brain health maintained (Steady Normal / Steady MCI): Predicted MRIs at 3-year intervals generated from the current MRI reflecting aging factors only (axial, coronal, sagittal views), predicted MMSE score
  • Case 2 - Brain health not managed: Predicted MRIs at 3-year intervals generated from the current MRI reflecting aging and cognitive decline (Normal → MCI or MCI → AD), predicted MMSE score
useCases.brainRequiredDataDesc: Synthetic longitudinal MRIs and predicted MMSE scores conditioned on patient input information, covering three trajectories: brain health maintenance, Normal-to-MCI transition, and MCI-to-AD transition.
useCases.brainEval1Desc: Evaluating distributional similarity between real and synthetic brain images.
useCases.brainEval2Desc: Reproducibility of region-specific chronic brain changes.
useCases.breastOverview: From a patient's pre-operative MRI and the planned surgery type, the model predicts the post-operative breast. The synthesized image displays the resection region and the resulting shape change. This lets physicians analyze the expected outcome before surgery and patients take part in the treatment decision.
useCases.breastDataDesc: The generative model is trained using 1,483 cases of pre-operative and post-operative breast MR images. The framework incorporates structural knowledge including resection regions, implant location, and implant size as conditioning information.
useCases.breastStep1Desc: Raw DICOM files are automatically classified into clinically meaningful MRI series to support AI model input selection and user navigation.
useCases.breastStep2Desc: Breast MR images are standardized through orientation correction, N4-bias field removal, noise reduction, and normalization.
useCases.breastStep3Desc: Breast tissue, tumor regions, resection regions, and implant-related structures are identified for quantitative analysis and generation conditioning.
useCases.breastStep4Desc: Pre-operative and post-operative MR images are aligned to construct training data for longitudinal generative modeling.
useCases.breastStep5Desc: A generative model predicts post-surgical breast MRI from pre-operative MRI, conditioned on surgical plan, tumor location, resection region, and implant information.
useCases.breastStep6Desc: Generated results can be integrated into a medical twin-based surgical planning system with MRI visualization, segmentation, measurement, and 3D rendering modules.
useCases.breastEval1Desc: Turing tests in which breast cancer specialists compare synthetic and real post-surgical MR images.
useCases.breastEval2Desc: Agreement between generated and reference surgical regions (resection and implant) measured using Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC).
